import io
import string
import list

fn parse_ints(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  let n = string.len(s)
  mut i = 0
  while i < n
    mut done_ws = false
    while not done_ws
      if i >= n
        done_ws = true
      else
        match string.byte_at(s, i)
        when Some(b)
          if b == 32 or b == 9 or b == 10 or b == 13
            i = i + 1
          else
            done_ws = true
          end
        when None
          done_ws = true
        end
      end
    end
    if i < n
      mut neg = false
      match string.byte_at(s, i)
      when Some(b)
        if b == 45
          neg = true
          i = i + 1
        else if b == 43
          i = i + 1
        end
      when None
        ()
      end
      mut val = 0
      mut done_num = false
      while not done_num
        if i >= n
          done_num = true
        else
          match string.byte_at(s, i)
          when Some(c)
            if c >= 48 and c <= 57
              val = val * 10 + (c - 48)
              i = i + 1
            else
              done_num = true
            end
          when None
            done_num = true
          end
        end
      end
      if neg
        val = -val
      end
      result = list.push(result, val)
    end
  end
  result
end

fn main() -> Unit
  let line1 = match io.read_line()
  when Some(s)
    s
  when None
    ""
  end
  let line2 = match io.read_line()
  when Some(s)
    s
  when None
    ""
  end
  let nums = parse_ints(line1)
  let k = match string.parse_int(string.trim(line2))
  when Some(v)
    v
  when None
    0
  end
  let n = nums.len()
  let limit = if k > n
    n
  else
    k
  end
  mut used: List<Int> = []
  mut p = 0
  while p < n
    used = list.push(used, 0)
    p = p + 1
  end
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  mut count = 0
  while count < limit
    mut best_idx = -1
    mut best_val = 0
    mut i = 0
    while i < n
      if used[i] == 0
        if best_idx == -1
          best_idx = i
          best_val = nums[i]
        else if nums[i] > best_val
          best_idx = i
          best_val = nums[i]
        end
      end
      i = i + 1
    end
    mut new_used: List<Int> = []
    mut j = 0
    while j < n
      if j == best_idx
        new_used = list.push(new_used, 1)
      else
        new_used = list.push(new_used, used[j])
      end
      j = j + 1
    end
    used = new_used
    result = list.push(result, best_val)
    count = count + 1
  end
  mut out = ""
  mut q = 0
  while q < count
    if q == 0
      out = "#{result[q]}"
    else
      out = "#{out} #{result[q]}"
    end
    q = q + 1
  end
  println(out)
end
